title: Peltopus alveatus
taxonomy: Peltopus alveatus is classified as a species of sea snail within the family Muricidae. This family consists of large sea snails commonly known as murex snails. Muricidae is a diverse group of marine gastropod mollusks, with over 1,500 species found in oceans worldwide. The classification of Peltopus alveatus places it within the phylum Mollusca and the kingdom Animalia. Further classification includes the domain Eukarya and the class Gastropoda.

The health benefits of Peltophorum alveatum include its use as an anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial agent, which may help to treat various conditions such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections.
